visual_checks: Look for a picture-in-picture (PiP) window showing a sign language interpreter alongside the video
Check if the sign language interpretation is synchronized with the spoken audio content
Verify the interpreter is clearly visible and not obscured by video content or UI controls
Check if the interpreter window is large enough to see hand shapes, facial expressions, and body movements
Look for a toggle or option to enable/disable the sign language interpretation track
Check if the sign language interpretation covers all spoken dialogue and narration in the video
pass_conditions: A sign language interpreter is provided in a PiP window or separate synchronized video
The interpretation is synchronized with the spoken audio content
The interpreter is clearly visible with sufficient size to convey hand shapes, facial expressions, and body movements
All spoken dialogue and narration in the prerecorded video is interpreted
The sign language interpretation does not obstruct essential video content
fail_conditions: Prerecorded video with audio has no sign language interpretation provided
Sign language interpretation is present but not synchronized with the audio
The interpreter window is too small to clearly see signs and facial expressions
Only portions of the spoken content are interpreted, leaving gaps
The interpreter is obscured by video controls or overlapping content
na_conditions: Page contains no prerecorded video with audio content
Page contains only audio-only or video-only (no synchronized media) content
The media is live rather than prerecorded
ne_conditions: AI response could not be parsed
Screenshots could not be captured for visual verification
Video content failed to load so sign language interpretation cannot be evaluated
common_mistakes: Providing captions but no sign language interpretation, assuming captions alone satisfy this criterion
Sign language interpreter window too small or positioned where video controls overlap it
Interpretation that covers only the main dialogue but omits narration or off-screen speech
No option to toggle sign language track on or off for user preference
Interpreter video that is out of sync with the main audio track

examples.pass: A training video includes a picture-in-picture window in the lower-right corner showing a sign language interpreter who interprets all spoken dialogue and narration in sync with the audio
examples.fail: A prerecorded webinar video has captions but no sign language interpreter is provided in any form
examples.partial: A sign language interpreter is provided for the main presentation but not for the Q&A portion of the recorded video

plain_meaning: At Level AAA, prerecorded synchronized media must offer sign language interpretation of the audio. The intent is to serve deaf users whose first language is a sign language and for whom written captions in a spoken language are a second-language experience. This is a high bar and is rarely met outside of specialized accessible-content initiatives.
scope_applies_to: Prerecorded synchronized media with meaningful audio content, when the site is claiming Level AAA conformance
Video lectures, announcements, and formal communications aiming for AAA
Embedded or self-hosted prerecorded video with audio
scope_does_not_apply_to: Prerecorded audio-only or video-only content -- that belongs to SC 1.2.1
Sites not claiming Level AAA conformance (criterion is AAA)
Audio description -- that belongs to SC 1.2.3 / 1.2.5
Media that is a media alternative for text on the same page and is clearly labeled as such (general media-alternative exemption)
Keyboard or focus issues on the player (SC 2.1.1 / 2.4.7)
borderline_calibration: This is AAA. Only flag when the site claims AAA conformance or when the task explicitly asks for AAA evaluation.
A picture-in-picture signer or a side-by-side signer both satisfy the rule.
Sign language embedded in the original video counts; no separate track is required.
Missing sign language interpretation on AAA-scope prerecorded video is a hard failure at medium+ within AAA context; outside AAA, mark not-applicable.
